insight: Checker Grammar runs BEFORE SoundNotify.addTagAndSound in the chat pipeline, so `Grammar.Insert_Dot_Message_Length` appends a `.` after a trailing `@mention`, breaking the SoundNotify regex boundary and silently disabling the mention highlight/sound. Workaround: set `Grammar.Insert_Dot_Message_Length: 0` or grant `chatcontrol.bypass.grammar`. Capitalization is harmless because the mention regex is case-insensitive.
